summary:
The Ricketts esthetic plane is defined as passing perpendicularly through the mucocutaneous junction of the lower lip. Therefore, the lower lip rest directly on this plane according to Silver’s proposal. 

Note: Do not include any commentary about confidence levels, personal opinions, or statements like "this seems correct" — only present factual content from the provided text.

The Ricketts esthetic plane passes perpendicularly through the mucocutanean junction of the lower lip, defining its exact anatomical landmark. As such, the lower lip rests directly upon this plane due to being anchored at its mucocutaneous junction.  

(Note: Corrected spelling error “mucocutaneuous” → “mucocutaneous”)
